title: Hopea pedicellata
taxonomy: Hopea pedicellata is a species of plant in the family Dipterocarpaceae. This plant is classified within the genus Hopea, which consists of species of evergreen trees found in Southeast Asia. The family Dipterocarpaceae is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the "dipterocarps" and are native to tropical Asia and Malaysia. Dipterocarpaceae is further classified in the order Malvales and the class Magnoliopsida. The classification of Hopea pedicellata provides insight into its evolutionary relationships and botanical characteristics.

intro: Hopea pedicellata, also known as East Indian Hopea, is a tropical tree species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Dipterocarpaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and skin diseases, thanks to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Hopea pedicellata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its pharmacological activities. The most common herbal preparations made with Hopea pedicellata include decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and fever.
